title: Sacred Heart Syro-Malabar Cathedral
tagline: Sacred Heart Syro-Malabar Cathedral — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: The Sacred Heart Cathedral is a cathedral of the Syro-Malabar Catholic rite in Rajkot.
overview.description: The Sacred Heart Cathedral is a cathedral of the Syro-Malabar Catholic rite in Rajkot. It is also known as Prem Mandir, meaning 'temple of love' in Hindi and Gujarati. The cathedral, which is dedicated to the Sacred Heart of Jesus, is situated near the bishop's house. The striking aspect of the cathedral is its architecture which predominantly resembles a traditional Indian temple, but in fact blends architectural features of various religions.

topPlaces.name: Eparchy of Rajkot
topPlaces.description: The Eparchy of Rajkot is an Eastern Catholic eparchy in Gujarat, India, under the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church. It was created in 1977.

topPlaces.name: Crystal Mall Rajkot
topPlaces.description: Crystal Mall is a shopping mall located in Rajkot in Gujarat State of India. It is located on western part of Rajkot City on main Kalawad Road.

topPlaces.name: 2024 Rajkot gaming zone fire
topPlaces.description: On 25 May 2024, a fire broke out at a gaming zone in Rajkot, Gujarat, India, killing 32 people.

topPlaces.name: Rajkot
topPlaces.description: Rajkot (Hindi: [ˈraːdʒkoːʈ] ) is the fourth-largest city in the western Indian state of Gujarat, after Ahmedabad, Surat, and Vadodara. It is in the centre of the Saurashtra region of Gujarat.

topPlaces.name: Rajkot State
topPlaces.description: Rajkot State was one of the princely states of India during the period of British rule. It was a 9-gun salute state belonging to the Kathiawar Agency of the Bombay Presidency.

topPlaces.name: Madhavrao Scindia Cricket Ground
topPlaces.description: Madhavrao Shindhia Cricket Ground also known as Municipal Corporation Ground or Racecourse Ground is a stadium located in Rajkot, Gujarat, India. The venue hosted bilateral ODIs between 1980s-2000s, however since the venue's main tenent Saurashtra Cricket Association (SCA) built its own stadium in other part of the Rajkot city, they organise all international cricket allocated them at there.

topPlaces.name: Kathiawar Agency
topPlaces.description: The Kathiawar Agency, on the Kathiawar peninsula in the western part of the British Raj was a political unit of some 200 small princely states under the suzerainty of the Bombay Presidency. The agency's headquarters were at Rajkot, the town where the Political Agent used to reside.

topPlaces.name: Mahatma Gandhi Museum, Rajkot
topPlaces.description: Mahatma Gandhi Museum was formerly Alfred High School (also known as Mohandas Gandhi High School or Kathiawar High School) in Rajkot was one of the oldest educational institutions in India which was active for 164 years where Mahatma Gandhi studied for a few years.

items.note: The Eparchy of Rajkot is an Eastern Catholic eparchy in Gujarat, India, under the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church. It was created in 1977.
Crystal Mall is a shopping mall located in Rajkot in Gujarat State of India. It is located on western part of Rajkot City on main Kalawad Road.
On 25 May 2024, a fire broke out at a gaming zone in Rajkot, Gujarat, India, killing 32 people.
Rajkot (Hindi: [ˈraːdʒkoːʈ] ) is the fourth-largest city in the western Indian state of Gujarat, after Ahmedabad, Surat, and Vadodara. It is in the centre of the Saurashtra region of Gujarat.
Rajkot State was one of the princely states of India during the period of British rule. It was a 9-gun salute state belonging to the Kathiawar Agency of the Bombay Presidency.

Madhavrao Shindhia Cricket Ground also known as Municipal Corporation Ground or Racecourse Ground is a stadium located in Rajkot, Gujarat, India. The venue hosted bilateral ODIs between 1980s-2000s, however since the venue's main tenent Saurashtra Cricket Association (SCA) built its own stadium in other part of the Rajkot city, they organise all international cricket allocated them at there.

The Kathiawar Agency, on the Kathiawar peninsula in the western part of the British Raj was a political unit of some 200 small princely states under the suzerainty of the Bombay Presidency. The agency's headquarters were at Rajkot, the town where the Political Agent used to reside.

Mahatma Gandhi Museum was formerly Alfred High School (also known as Mohandas Gandhi High School or Kathiawar High School) in Rajkot was one of the oldest educational institutions in India which was active for 164 years where Mahatma Gandhi studied for a few years.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sacred Heart Syro-Malabar Cathedral?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sacred Heart Syro-Malabar Cathedral is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sacred Heart Syro-Malabar Cathedral?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajkot Airport (4 km) and the nearest railway station is Rajkot Junction (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sacred Heart Syro-Malabar Cathedral?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sacred Heart Syro-Malabar Cathedral at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sacred Heart Syro-Malabar Cathedral?
faq.a: Stays in Sacred Heart Syro-Malabar Cathedral start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sacred Heart Syro-Malabar Cathedral?
faq.a: The top attractions are Eparchy of Rajkot, Crystal Mall Rajkot, 2024 Rajkot gaming zone fire, Rajkot, Rajkot State.
